rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned values.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference features or variables that are visible in the current component scope.
When generating AGREE code, ensure all types used in constants and equations match AGREE's supported scalar types: int, real, bool.
Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes; use implementation scopes for such constructs.
If a guarantee or equation references an invalid or unresolved identifier, remove the clause rather than adding missing architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions; avoid package-qualified names unless explicitly imported.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new component ports or subcomponents in AGREE contracts unless they are already defined in the AADL model.
Do not write guarantees in implementation scopes; use type scopes for output dependency contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
